Commercial Linkage Fees (CLF) are a standard tool used by local communities for generating affordable housing resources. The CLF is similar to other impact fees levied on new development, and helps cover the cost associated with creating new or expanded public facilities to meet the additional demand created by the development, such as parks, schools, libraries and streets. Before levying an impact fee, jurisdictions are required by state law to complete a nexus study that shows the linkage between the new development and the increased demand for the facilities. The results of the nexus study establish the maximum legal fee that may be charged.

In the case of the CLF, as cities build jobs-g

ranked_candidates.snippet: The San José City Council voted on September 1, 2020 to begin charging commercial developers fees to fund affordable housing. The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use

Ft. ; effective_date=October 1, 2013 ; adoption_date=May 15, 2008 ; final_status=adopted ; source_locator=reader_content:1:fee_table_row ; table_locator=commercial_linkage_fee_table ; locator_quality=table_row_chunk_locator ; source_ref=https://www.hcd.ca.gov/housing-elements/docs/los altos_5th_draft011415.pdf ; source_family=meeting_minutes ; confidence=0.86]

The San José City Council voted on September 1, 2020 to begin charging commercial developers fees to fund affordable housing. The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use. The San José City Council voted on December 11, 2018 to pursue a nexus and a feasibility study on a potential commercial linkage fee in San José. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reducti [normalized=3.58 ; unit=usd_per_square_foot ; source_family=tavily_secondary_search]

top_results.snippet: The San José City Council voted on September 1, 2020 to begin charging commercial developers fees to fund affordable housing. The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use. The San José City Council voted on December 11, 2018 to pursue a nexus and a feasibility study on a potential commercial linkage fee in San José. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply. | Industrial/Research and Development(≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply.
top_results.title: Commercial Linkage Fee | City of San José

top_results.snippet: Before levying an impact fee, jurisdictions are required by state law to complete a nexus study that shows the linkage between the new development and the increased demand for the facilities. In the case of the CLF, as cities build jobs-generating commercial real estate – like new office buildings, retail space, or warehouses – commercial developers pay a fee to offset a small portion of the need for affordable housing created by new jobs. Following the nexus study, local city councils set fees at levels expected to generate resources for local affordable housing development without making commercial development infeasible. On Tuesday, September 1st, 2020, the San Jose City Council approved a Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Ordinance, joining all of the other major cities in the South Bay that have already adopted fees. ### City of San Jose Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance - September 1, 2020. * City of Mountain View 2013 Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance. * City of Sunnyvale 2015 Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance.
top_results.title: Commercial Linkage Fees - SV@Home

top_results.snippet: ## Update on Commercial Linkage Fee - Nexus and Feasibility Reports Issued, Public Meeting 8/6/2020. ## Update - August 4, 2020 Public Meeting on Commercial Linkage Fee. On Friday July 17, 2020, the City published the nexus study pertaining to the Commercial Linkage Fee study and staff is releasing the feasibility study this week. wo key studies on the implications of a Commercial Linkage Fee have been completed, the Nexus Analysis and the Feasibility Analysis. The Nexus Analysis has been prepared for the limited purpose of determining nexus support for a potential new commercial linkage fee in San José. The Feasibility Analysis assesses the economic effects of potential commercial linkage fees and provides context materials to support selection of equity fee levels for a new commercial linkage fee program for the City of San José. Staff will bring the Commercial Linkage Fee forward for Council consideration on August 25, 2020.
top_results.title: Update on Commercial Linkage Fee - Nexus and Feasibility Reports Issued, Public Meeting 8/6/2020 | Blog | City of San José
